reasoning: The news that the UK joined France's initiative for the recognition of a Palestinian state around May 20, 2025, acts as a very strong signal of imminent diplomatic action. Similar movements in 2024 (Spain, Ireland, Norway in May; Slovenia in June) demonstrate that when Western European states begin openly coordinating on recognition, actual formal announcements typically follow within a matter of weeks. While there is a risk that the formal announcement could either be rushed before the end of May or delayed until later in the summer (or even September around the UN General Assembly), June 2025 represents the most probable timeframe for either the UK, France, or other likely European candidates (such as Belgium, Malta, or Luxembourg) to formally jump on board and make an announcement. The sheer weight of two P2/G7 nations initiating this process creates immense momentum and pressure, making it highly probable (around 68%) that at least one UN member country will announce recognition in June.
